schema_json.description: An expert answer grounded in search results.

key_findings.description: 3-5 key findings extracted from search results
key_findings.title: Key Findings

sources_used.description: URLs of sources referenced in the answer
sources_used.title: Sources Used

schema_json.title: StormAnswer

prompt:
You are a research expert being interviewed about:
RESEARCH QUESTION: What are the proven health benefits and risks of intermittent fasting in adults with metabolic syndrome?

The interviewer (Rajiv Menon, MBA, Industry perspective) asks:
QUESTION: Round 2 revealed that the JMIR 52-week LIFE app study showed 13.9% weight loss in BMI≥40 users but slight weight GAIN in lower-BMI users. Two industry questions follow: (1) What self-reported adverse events, medical complications, or concerning patterns — hypoglycemia, fainting, eating disorder relapses, electrolyte disturbances, reported hospitalizations — have been documented in the real-world IF app literature or post-market surveillance by commercial IF app vendors? (2) Has any IF app adjusted its marketing, onboarding screening, or protocol recommendations in response to the BMI-dependent benefit pattern, or are they still marketed uniformly to all users regardless of baseline BMI or metabolic risk?

SEARCH RESULTS (your ONLY source of information):
[Source 1] National Eating Disorders Association: Home
  URL: https://www.nationaleatingdisorders.org/
  Content: Visit the National Eating Disorders Association (NEDA) to learn about eating disorders, find help, and how you can take action to raise awareness.

  Content: In this issue of BMC Clinical Diabetes and Endocrinology, Albosta et al. present a comprehensive review of the current literature as well as a ...

  URL: https://emilyprogram.com/blog/is-intermittent-fasting-dangerous/
  Content: Intermittent fasting is a restrictive behavior and is worrying in the ways it overlaps with eating disorders like anorexia, bulimia, binge ...
[Source 4] Achieving Type 2 Diabetes Remission in Real-World Settings with ...
  URL: https://agmr.hapres.com/htmls/AGMR_1737_Detail.html
  Content: Type 2 diabetes (T2D) remission, defined as an A1c level below 6.5% without the use of glucose-lowering medications for at least three months, ...
[Source 5] Impact of Ramadan Diurnal Intermittent Fasting on Hypoglycemic ...
  URL: https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/endocrinology/articles/10.3389/fendo.2021.624423/full
  Content: Given that many patients with diabetes observe fasting during Ramadan, glycemic control management for those patients is a critically important health challenge ...
[Source 6] The Safety and Efficacy of Intermittent Fasting for Weight Loss
  URL: https://www.researchgate.net/publication/346446238_The_Safety_and_Efficacy_of_Intermittent_Fasting_for_Weight_Loss
  Content: Defined IF regimens include 5 different types: alternate-day fasting, periodic fasting, time-restricted feeding, less clearly defined IF (fast mimicking diet, ...
[Source 7] Myths about intermittent fasting, debunked
  URL: https://ahs.uic.edu/news-stories/myths-about-intermittent-fasting-debunked/
  Content: Intermittent fasting has become an increasingly popular way to lose weight without counting calories. And a large body of research has shown it's safe.
[Source 8] Effects of 4:3 Intermittent Fasting on Eating Behaviors and Appetite ...
  URL: https://www.mdpi.com/2072-6643/17/14/2385
  Content: Background/Objectives: Daily caloric restriction (DCR) is a common dietary weight loss strategy, but leads to metabolic and behavioral adaptations, ...
[Source 9] Intermittent fasting is safe, effective for those with Type 2 diabetes
  URL: https://www.eurekalert.org/news-releases/1005788
  Content: Time-restricted eating, also known as intermittent fasting, can help people with Type 2 diabetes lose weight and control their blood sugar levels.
[Source 10] Intermittent Fasting and Disordered Eating: Expert Q&A - WebMD
  URL: https://www.webmd.com/mental-health/eating-disorders/features/when-does-intermittent-fasting-slip-into-disordered-eating
  Content: Intermittent fasting is popular for health and weight loss, but there can be a risk of disordered eating or eating disorders.

  Content: With Zero's expert guidance and simple intermittent fasting tracker, you'll unlock the full benefits of intermittent fasting to lose weight and burn fat faster.

  URL: https://fastic.com/en
  Content: Fastic supports your body's innate ability to shed excess weight in a holistic and sustainable way. Say goodbye to yo-yo dieting and welcome a new, balanced ...

  URL: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/fastic-weight-loss-fasting/id1459260306
  Content: Unlock your weight loss journey with Fastic, the app that tailors fasting and nutrition to fit YOUR lifestyle and goals. Dive into a personalized health ...

  URL: https://theintellify.com/develop-intermittent-fasting-apps-like-zero/

  Content: Get personalized insights based on your lifestyle. ✓ AI-POWERED ASSISTANCE: Got questions? Our AI chatbot, Fasty, provides instant answers and helpful guidance.

INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Provide a comprehensive, well-structured answer (200-400 words).
2. ONLY use information from the search results above. Do NOT add information not present in the sources.
3. When citing specific facts, mention which source they come from.
4. Extract 3-5 key findings as bullet points.
5. List the URLs of sources you actually used in your answer.
6. If the search results do not adequately answer the question, say so explicitly and note what information is missing.

Return a JSON object with 'answer', 'key_findings', and 'sources_used' fields.
